volumeTitle: Antigone. The Women of Trachis. Philoctetes. Oedipus at Colonus
label: Oedipus at Colonus (Section 2.1744a - Section 1777)

sections.label: Section 2.1745
sections.original: τοτὲ μὲν ἄπορα , τοτὲ δʼ ὕπερθεν .
sections.translation: Then it was desperate , but now even crueler .

sections.label: Section 2.1746
sections.translation: Vast , then , is the sea of your troubles .

sections.label: Section 2.1747
sections.translation: Alas , alas ! Zeus , where shall we turn ?

sections.label: Section 2.1750
sections.translation: To what last hope does the divinity now drive us ?

sections.label: Section 1751
sections.original: παύετε θρήνων , παῖδες · ἐν οἷς γὰρ
sections.translation: Cease your lament , children ! Where the favor of the nether night is stored up , there is no room for sorrow ; divine retribution would follow .

sections.label: Section 1754
sections.translation: Son of Aegeus , we supplicate you !

sections.label: Section 1755
sections.translation: To obtain what desire , my children ?

sections.label: Section 1756
sections.original: τύμβον θέλομεν προσιδεῖν αὐταὶ πατρὸς ἡμετέρου .
sections.translation: We want look with our own eyes upon our father ’ s tomb .

sections.label: Section 1758
sections.translation: It is not right to go there .

sections.label: Section 1760
sections.translation: Children , he told me that no one should draw near that place , or approach with prayer the sacred tomb in which he sleeps . He said that , so long as I saw to this , I would always keep the country free from pain .

sections.label: Section 1765
sections.translation: The divinity heard me say these things , as did the all-seeing Oath of Zeus .

sections.label: Section 1768
sections.original: ἀλλʼ εἰ τάδʼ ἔχει κατὰ νοῦν κείνῳ ,
sections.translation: If this is his intention , we must be content with it .

sections.label: Section 1770
sections.translation: Send us to ancient Thebes , in case we may somehow stop the bloodshed that threatens our brothers .

sections.label: Section 1773
sections.original: δράσω καὶ τάδε καὶ πάνθʼ ὁπόσʼ ἂν
sections.translation: I will do both this and whatever other favorable service I can , for you

sections.label: Section 1775
sections.original: καὶ τῷ κατὰ γῆς , ὃς νέον ἔρρει ,
sections.translation: and for the newly-departed under the earth , according to the gratitude I owe . I am bound to spare no pains .

sections.label: Section 1777
sections.translation: Cease ; raise up the lamentation no further . These things are established firm and fixed .
